title: "I want Angelina lips" - How to manage celeb expectations

excerpt: And i'll say and obviously you're not going to look exactly like them but we're going to use it we're going to be using those uh her lips as inspiration give me that give me some angelina jolie lips so i'd say so in terms of what you like about these lips what do you what is it t…

segments.text: and i'll say and obviously you're not going to look exactly like them but we're going to use it we're going to be using those uh her lips as inspiration give me that give me some angelina jolie lips so i'd say so in terms of what you like about these lips what do you what is it that stands out to you nice is it so because what you're always looking for with the celebrity picture is something a communication most very few people really expect they're gonna look like angelina jolie what they're saying is take me in this direction and that's an another great tip is that you should always talk about relative changes not absolute changes i'm not going to make you look like angelina jolie but i can take what you're communicating this image and use it to inform my treatment design to make you a little bit closer to angelina jolie so you know you might say well look at her tubercles i can see that's what you like i can see you have essence of that but i can make them a little bit fuller that'll shift you in that direction and you know i quite often assume they know they're not going to look like angie angeline jolie and i'll say and

obviously you're not going to look exactly like them but we're going to use it we're going to be using those her lips as inspiration and that therefore they feel listened to you can then say well i won't be able to get it as good as this in this area or as big or as full or as whatever shape is you you're trying to so you you're modifying what you you're telling what you can't do and telling them what you can do and using the image that they're using to try and communicate what's a very difficult thing to communicate

transcript: and i'll say and obviously you're not going to look exactly like them but we're going to use it we're going to be using those uh her lips as inspiration give me that give me some angelina jolie lips so i'd say so in terms of what you like about these lips what do you what is it that stands out to you nice is it so because what you're always looking for with the celebrity picture is something a communication most very few people really expect they're gonna look like angelina jolie what they're saying is take me in this direction and that's an another great tip is that you should always talk about relative changes not absolute changes i'm not going to make you look like angelina jolie but i can take what you're communicating this image and use it to inform my treatment design to make you a little bit closer to angelina jolie so you know you might say well look at her tubercles i can see that's what you like i can see you have essence of that but i can make them a little bit fuller that'll shift you in that direction and you know i quite often assume they know they're not going to look like angie angeline jolie and i'll say and obviously you're not going to look exactly like them but we're going to use it we're going to be using those her lips as inspiration and that therefore they feel listened to you can then say well i won't be able to get it as good as this in this area or as big or as full or as whatever shape is you you're trying to so you you're modifying what you you're telling what you can't do and telling them what you can do and using the image that they're using to try and communicate what's a very difficult thing to communicate
